'''Corvanni IV''' was an astronomical object that occupied the fourth [[Orbit/Legends|orbital]] position<ref name="GG10" /> in the [[Corvanni system/Legends|Corvanni system]], a part of the [[Neshig sector/Legends|Neshig sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was located near the [[Vorsia system/Legends|Vorsia system]],<ref name="TEA" /> the origin point of the [[Vorsian crystal]].<ref name="Inferno">''[[Legacy of the Force: Inferno]]''</ref><ref name="Planetnamia">{{Planetnamia|other=the [[Vorsian crystal]]|system=[[Vorsia system/Legends|Vorsia system]]|legends=1}}</ref>

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], [[Gandalom City]] on Corvanni IV was the site of the corporate headquarters of the company [[Gandalom Paramedicinals, Inc.]]<ref name="GG10" /> At some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' is set between the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], which corresponds to some point between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the corporation posted a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Maxig Kress]], accusing them of trespassing on GPI property, attacking [[Security guard/Legends|security guards]], and [[Thief/Legends|stealing]] several experimental [[Crystal/Legends|crystalline]] compounds. In the bounty description, GPI indicated that, upon capture, both Kress and the compounds should be delivered to the company's headquarters on Corvanni IV.<ref name="GG10" />

==Behind the scenes==
Corvanni IV was introduced in the [[1994]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', which was written by [[Rick D. Stuart]] for [[West End Games]].<ref name="GG10" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Corvanni [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Corvanni IV, in grid square N-9.<ref name="TEA" />
